description_long: This is a vintage, hand-cranked grinder with a rectangular wooden frame and a cylindrical metal drum at its center. The grinder stands on four sturdy wooden legs, with a triangular support structure on the sides for stability. A prominent metal crank handle with a wooden grip extends from one side, used to rotate the drum for grinding. The drum itself is made of galvanized or weathered steel, showing some rust and wear, and is partially enclosed by the wooden frame. The top of the grinder features a flat, lidded compartment, likely for loading material to be ground. The overall construction suggests durability and a utilitarian design, suitable for outdoor or garage use in an urban environment.
description: A vintage, hand-cranked grinder with a wooden frame, metal drum, and a side handle, supported by four legs and a triangular brace. The top has a lidded compartment and the drum is partially enclosed by wood.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rectangular wooden frame, the metal drum partially visible, and the four supporting legs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the circular end of the drum, the triangular wooden support, and the metal crank handle with a wooden grip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the crank handle fully visible, showing its attachment to the drum and the wooden frame.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the circular end of the drum and the triangular support structure, with no handle visible.
attribute_handle_design: A single, straight metal crank handle with a cylindrical wooden grip is mounted on the side of the drum. The handle is simple, with no visible ergonomic texturing or auxiliary features.
